The Business Launch Part 4
!
I know you love examples, so I wanted to give you one more Business Launch Formula
example.
!
Look at those first two I gave you. They’re fundamentally different, but still there were
the common themes. Starting by building that list, continue to build that list, creating
products on what the market wants, creating a portfolio of products over time, not
getting it perfect the first time, starting small and growing from there.
!
I could give you dozens and dozens of examples and I've mentioned Susan Garrett. She
started off with that ebook, that $27,000 launch, which was great but she has grown
from there. She’s had one great product after another. We see this over and over.
!
It was in my Platinum group. The great thing about my Platinum group is I meet with
them three times a year, so I get to really dig inside their businesses. We see this
example over and over and over where people are just building a list, do a launch then
have their next product defined by the interaction they have with both their prospects
and their buyers lists. It works over and over.
!
There is almost never a big master plan to start with. It’s building the list, building the
relationship, listening to what people want. This next example is for my Product Launch
Formula business, where I teach people about online marketing. This is my biggest and
best business yet. I’m sure I’m probably going to have another one, but this one is
doing pretty well.
!
In 2003, I was still publishing about the stock market. I went to a marketing seminar. I
met a bunch of people, and thought, “This is really cool.” A lot of the people I met were
selling online marketing stuff. By that time I had been selling stuff for seven years, and
had built up a lot of experiences getting pretty good at marketing.
!
I knew I was pretty sure about marketing. I looked around and saw these people in
that business and thought, “This is a pretty cool business. I might want to be in that
business teaching online business. I might want to be in that business at some point.”
!
I built up some great relationships at that first seminar. I went home and I put together
a squeeze page and I started doing a little tiny list building, very, very humble. I think in
the next couple of years I got between 1,000 and 2,000 people on that list. It was
nothing impression, especially given the numbers I had in that stock market niche
where I was publishing to this huge list.
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 2
PLF_Module1v1.0
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 2 of 6 --

!
I had a significant business making a bunch of money. I just put up a squeeze page and
let traffic trickle into that squeeze page. I didn’t really do anything to drive traffic to it,
just built it through word of mouth.
!
I started to build relationships. Every now and then I would mail about an affiliate off,
but maybe twice a year maximum. That was my thinking, “I might want to be in that
business someday. I don’t know. I might want to be there some day. I’m just going to
start publishing. We’ll see what happens.”
!
I wrote an autoresponder sequence. In fact the site is still up. It’s at SixInSeven.com.
It’s ugly, ugly, ugly. You can see what my sites looked like back in 2003. Since I’m
putting this out there, maybe I’ll go and put a decent page up at SixInSeven.com.
That’s for six figures in seven days.
!
When I first went to that seminar, I started telling people about my launches. I had just
come off of doing that six figure launch. It seemed really cool in the day. It still is really
cool, but in this era where I’m doing these million dollar launches and my friends are
doing million dollar launches, it’s almost quaint. Back then it was really impressive.
!
I shortened that six figures in seven days to SixInSeven.com and I put up the squeeze
page and started building the list. I only mention that because this is more than two
and a half years before I would ever actually sell anything in that market. My first
thought was, “I might want to play in this market at some point. I’m going to create a
squeeze page.”
!
I wrote some autoresponder copy and that was it. I didn’t do anything to it what so
ever. Like I said, in two and a half years I maybe sent out three promotions total, I
think. Then an interesting thing happened in March, 2005. My partnership broke up.
!
You know that happens to partnerships, but this happened rather suddenly. One day I
had a business. I got a phone call, and picked up the phone. My partner didn’t want to
be in business anymore so that business went away.
!
That was in early 2005. I was scheduled to speak at Yanik’s Underground Online
seminar. Yanik is one of the friends I had made. Yanik Silver was one of the prominent
internet marketing trainers at the time. I had gotten to know him and become friends.
He had asked me to speak at an event and I said, “Sure, I’ll come out and speak.”
!
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 3
PLF_Module2v1.0
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 3 of 6 --

That was sort of my coming out party, to go out and speak at this marketing event. I
think it was at the end of March, 2005 when the event was going to be. I was planning
on just going, speaking, walking off the stage and not doing anything. Then when that
business blew up about three weeks before that I thought, “Huh, maybe I’ll do a
training about these product launches. Maybe I’ll put together a training and sell the
training from stage.”
!
That’s what I did. There were 200 or 300 people in the room. I got up there and for an
hour and 25 minutes I gave the best content that I had. I taught everything I knew
about product launches. At the end of it I said, “If you want to learn more about this,
you want to get some more training, you can get my course. I’m putting together a
training for this for $3000.” I had six people buy.
!
$18,000 at that point was great because my business had gone away earlier that
month, so it was great to bring in that money, but really selling to six people when
there are 200 to 300 people in the room was really lousy results. It was because I don’t
know how to sell. I wasn’t a sales person. I wasn’t a pitch man.
!
I took those six people at $3000 each. That was my initial seed launch for Product
Launch Formula. I called it the Product Launch Workshop. What I did was, I took those
six people and thought, “That’s not enough to teach, because I’m going to do a series
of calls.”
!
I had made a bunch of friends now, and I had a mastermind group and I invited them
all in on a comp basis. They didn’t have to pay. We added about 30 more people in on a
comp basis. We ended up with 36 people in this first training. Six paid, 30 for free, but
it gave me enough to do the training. There might have been more. There might have
been 40 or whatever.
!
We had this good training. I promised them five teleseminars. I delivered those
teleseminars. The key thing is through each teleseminar, I kept going back to them and
saying, “Okay, on that topic (maybe the topic was prelaunch content) what questions
do you have? What didn’t I cover? What do you need to know? What did I do really well
on that call? What do I need to do differently? How do I need to train you better?” I did
that after every call.
!
The feedback I got basically taught me how to teach product launches. I knew how to
do product launches. I had done a whole bunch of them. At that point in 2005 I had
done dozens of them, but I’d never taught it.
!
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 4
PLF_Module1v1.0
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 4 of 6 --

This is how I learned to teach. This is a key principle of the seed launch which we’re
going to be getting to in this module. That’s how I learned. This is the Product Launch
Workshop (PLW).
!
I was thinking, “This is working great. I worked with these people. They got some great
results. Since it was so interactive they loved it. I did a great job with it because it was
interactive. I got great word of mouth. I got some great case studies out of that.”
I had case studies, and I’m thinking, “I’m liking this business. I’m liking the potential
here, even though I only sold six.” I knew it because I was a lousy sales person. I
wasn’t experienced at selling from stage. What I did was I created version 1.0 of
Product Launch Formula (PLF).
!
I had that tiny little list I’d started building. I had about 2,000 people on that tiny little
list, but I also had all of these JV partners. The JV partners were all those friendships I
had made in the prior two years, all the relationships. Because I had this great word of
mouth coming out of these people, and because I had been building all of these
relationships, they were all interested in promoting.
!
I sent my 2,000 people into the prelaunch. My JVs all sent their people. All of a sudden
I think I came out of this with about between 15,000 and 20,000 people on my list that
I built through this launch, just by these JVs. That was just absolutely amazing.
This 1.0 launch did $600,000. I went on to do much bigger launches. Certainly launches
in this business are much bigger now, but that was one of the biggest launches ever at
that point.
!
I was brand new to the market. No one knew who I was. I had built some relationships
with some powerful joint venture partners. I had some friendships from going to that
seminar and I got to a few other seminars.
!
I had some good friendships in the inner circle, but for the most part the public didn’t
know who I was, and I was still able to come out and do $600,000. Primarily, it was
because I had done this initial workshop and I’d learned how to teach it.
!
One of the things I want to mention is, I had recorded all of those calls, all of the bonus
calls, but when I did this version 1.0 I ended up going and recreating the video product
because I really wanted a great product. I used what I had learned about how to teach
it.
!
This evolved — I had done $600,000. I kept the product on the market. I was doing
about $1 million in sales per year. Then in 2008 I came out with 2.0. I leveraged all of
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 5
PLF_Module2v1.0
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 5 of 6 --

the list build. I leveraged all those JV’s, and I leveraged everything that I had learned in
those three years. This one did $3.7 million in 36 hours that we were open, completely
crushed it.
!
I also did some, what we call, line extensions added to the portfolio. In here I did a
coaching program and I did a live event. There were a couple of different levels to the
coaching program. The highest level was $18,000 for a three month event. That was
just based on the positioning I had got off of this 1.0 launch.
!
The first peel off live workshop was $5000 ticket to get into that. I did those in here.
Then we came out with 2.0. Then a few years later we came out with 3.0. That one
again did almost $4 million. It was just about exactly the same. I think it was $3.6
million.
!
We did a Product Launch Manager that was another product that the market told us
that they wanted. That was $2.5 million. That was a $10,000 video workshop basically.
Then we did Platinum that came out of 3.0. I’ve got this amazing Platinum group. I love
these folks. That program has a few different levels, but the highest level is $32,000 a
year.
!
You can see again, it’s just very organic how one thing lead on to another. It basically
came out of me doing a seed launch. Seed launches are awesome. We’re about to get
to that in this module. The seed launch where I only had six people who bought. We
now have over 10,000 people have gone through PLF.
!
All of this based on selling six, learning how to teach, learning what the market wanted,
then walking through here, and continually listening to what the market wanted,
updating product, upgrading the product, creating additional products like PLM and Plat
and the live workshop. Here we are 10,000 people, half a billion dollars in launches
from my Product Launch Formula owners.
!
Again, you can start very humbly and it’s all about getting that first product out there,
and then iterating from there, making it better from there and walking forward.
!
